messages.content:
-	Discussed his travel plan (as below) (per the parameters that Addie sent to us), and he acknowledged that they are tentatively accurate:- 
check grammar
The grammar in this sentence is correct.

-	He has 2 children (22 and 18 years old, both are still under full education) and still his dependent. 
check grammar

The grammar in this sentence is correct. However, it could be made clearer and more concise by saying: "He has two dependent children who are currently in full-time education - one aged 22 and the other aged 18."

-	He has no property in HK and should be personally responsible for his HK tax. 
check grammar
The grammar in this sentence is correct.

-	From HK tax perspective, the year of transfer is 2023/24 assessment year, DTT HK will only be authorized for the year 2023/24. 
check grammar

The grammar in this sentence is correct, but it may be clearer if it is written as: "From a Hong Kong tax perspective, the year of transfer will be the 2023/24 assessment year and the Double Taxation Treaty for Hong Kong will only apply to that year."

-	From HK tax perspective, the year of transfer is 2023/24 assessment year, Deloitte HK will only be authorized for tax services for the year 2023/24.
check again

The grammar in this sentence is correct, but it can be rephrased for clarity and conciseness: "For Hong Kong tax purposes, the year of transfer is the 2023/24 assessment year, and Deloitte HK will only be authorized to provide tax services for that year."

-	He has no property in HK and should be personally responsible for his HK tax. 
check gramar
The grammar in this sentence is correct.

We have checked the GlobalAdvantage portal and note that you have yet to complete/submit AboutMe and GA Calendar
grammar ok?
The grammar in this sentence is correct.

We have checked the GlobalAdvantage portal and note that you have yet to complete/submit AboutMe and GA Calendar. An invitation should have been sent to you previously (see below), please log into your GlobalAdvantage account (www.gadeloitte.com) and click on the To Do tile to complete and submit of:-
grammar ok?

The grammar in this sentence is almost correct. Here's a corrected version: "We have checked the GlobalAdvantage portal and noticed that you have not yet completed/submitted AboutMe and GA Calendar. An invitation should have been sent to you previously (see below). Please log into your GlobalAdvantage account (www.gadeloitte.com) and click on the To Do tile to complete and submit the following:"

With the above in mind, there will be no tax due and no Notice of Assessment will be issued should the IRD agrees with our revised tax computation. 
check grammar
The grammar in this sentence is correct.
can you rephase

Here's a possible rephrased version for better clarity, "Provided the Inland Revenue Department agrees with our revised tax computation, no tax will be due, and no Notice of Assessment will be issued."
again

Sure! Here's another possible rephrased version that emphasizes the potential outcome: "If the Inland Revenue Department agrees with our revised tax computation, you will not owe any tax, and you will not receive a Notice of Assessment."
again

Certainly! Here's one more possible rephrased version that focuses on the fact that if the revised tax computation is accepted, no further action will be required: "If our revised tax computation is accepted by the Inland Revenue Department, you will not need to take any further action, and no tax will be due."

With the above in mind, if the IRD agrees with our revised tax computation, there will be no tax due and no Notice of Assessment will be issued. No further action will be required from you.
check gramma
The grammar in this sentence is correct.
rephrsae

It is possible to rephrase the sentence in a few different ways depending on what aspect of the information you want to emphasize. Here are some options:

1. "If the IRD agrees with our revised tax computation, no tax will be due, and no Notice of Assessment will be issued. This means that you won't need to take any further action."

2. "Provided that the IRD accepts our revised tax computation, you won't owe any tax, and you won't receive a Notice of Assessment. You can rest assured that there won't be any additional requirements on your end."

3. "Assuming that the IRD approves our revised tax computation, you won't be liable for any tax, and you won't get a Notice of Assessment. This means that you won't have to do anything else in regards to your Hong Kong taxes."

Should the IRD agrees with our revised tax computation, there will be no tax due and no Notice of Assessment will be issued. This means that no further action will be required from you.
check gramma
The grammar in this sentence is also correct.
